thesis_claim: The symptoms that define schizophrenia in the public mind — voices and delusions — are the ones that matter least for how a life actually goes; the negative and cognitive symptoms nobody pictures are what predict whether someone works, studies, and connects.
the_risk: Assert the asymmetry unhedged: positive symptoms own the public image but are the weakest predictor of functional outcome; negative + cognitive carry the weight.

block_plan.quote_verbatim: I was simply sectioned again, detached from my real self, observing what was being done to me in a third-person perspective. ... My thoughts, my emotions, and my actions, none of them belong to me any more.
block_plan.attribution: Kean C, 'Silencing the self: schizophrenia as a self-disturbance', Schizophrenia Bulletin 2009
block_plan.fallback: If the verbatim quote cannot be confirmed against PMC full text, render SCENE as third-person body copy citing Kean 2009 — the block does not ship without a resolved citation.

block_plan.content: A hallucination is a genuine perception, not imagination (Woods: voices are perceptual, heterogeneous); a delusion is a belief that survives disconfirming evidence (Coltheart two-factor).
Avolition, anhedonia, alogia, blunted affect, asociality — and why from outside they read as laziness, coldness, rudeness. Marder: five domains, key role in functional outcome.
Working memory, processing speed, executive function — largely invisible, rarely in the public picture. Grand mean impairment ~1 SD (g=-1.03); processing speed largest (g=-1.57).

One behaviour, two readings — inside account vs outside account of the same afternoon (e.g., a person who cancels plans: outside=rude/lazy; inside=avolition+cognitive fatigue). Tabs: 'What it looks like' / 'What it is'.

THE RISK. Positive symptoms own the public image and most of the treatment conversation; negative and cognitive symptoms predict functional outcome better. Green 1996: positive symptoms not associated with outcome in ANY study reviewed. Rabinowitz/CATIE: negative symptoms out-predict all others for functioning. State it; do not hedge.
The prodrome — what precedes onset, almost always recognised backwards. CHR transition ~25% by 3 years — an at-risk state whose meaning is usually clear only after.
The diagnosis is polythetic; two people carrying it may share almost no symptoms (Olbert: same diagnosis, possibly zero shared symptoms; combinations share <half).

block_plan.myth: Hallucinations in schizophrenia are always voices.
block_plan.fact: Auditory is most common (60-80%), but over half of patients hallucinate in more than one sense (53% multimodal); visual, tactile and other modalities are real.

block_plan.myth: People with schizophrenia have no insight into their illness.
block_plan.fact: Insight is variable and fluctuating, not simply absent — about half have moderate impairment, a quarter severe, and it shifts with symptom state.

block_plan.content: Short FAQ/accordion on the symptom domains.
